overview: The Public Works Committee approved several items including a recycled asphalt crushing contract with King Gravel for $126,900, a resolution supporting state transportation funding, established $100/day fines for sanitary sewer discharge violations, and approved construction agreements for Military Road and consulting services for the Alderson/Jelinek roundabout and Bladel Avenue projects. The committee also received updates on CIP projects including a $9 million grant award for Birch Street reconstruction.

agenda.item: Acknowledge April 2026 water and sewer permits
2025 Water Utility PSC annual report review

discussions.body: Michael reported the village received a Local Road Improvement Grant for Birch Street reconstruction between Schofield Avenue and Community Center Drive, originally a 2029 project now being moved to 2028. The grant covers over 50% of the estimated $1.5 million project cost. Staff noted over $9 million in grant funding received over the past 4-5 years. Discussion also covered Weston Avenue repairs by Integrity Grading and Excavating for settlement issues over the sanitary sewer trench, and upcoming Schofield Avenue repairs by HOS Construction.

Staff reported road limits were removed April 29th. Activities included mailbox repairs, sign repairs, dewatering operations along the Weston Avenue tributary for approximately one week, curb line sweeping, hot mix patching, and storm damage cleanup from East Nick. Culverts failed on Jones Street requiring replacement from two to three 18-inch culverts. Equipment maintenance and changeover from winter to summer operations was detailed.

Josh reported on a service repair at Caribou Coffee, well maintenance items, and significant clear water infiltration issues at lift stations. April showed 37 million gallons pumped into the system but 53-54 million gallons treated at wastewater plant - approximately 15 million gallons of clear water infiltration. Staff identified sump pump discharges to sanitary sewers as a major contributor. In-pipe Technologies will conduct an INI study to identify problem areas.

Michael presented the annual report showing operating revenues of $3 million, net operating income of $482,911 (consistent with 2024's $490,000), water loss at 6%, and utility value of nearly $34 million after infrastructure additions. PAS settlement funds of $1.44 million were received. Cash reserves stand at $6.8 million. Motion to acknowledge passed unanimously.

Michael explained this League of Wisconsin Municipalities resolution supports long-term transportation funding solutions at the state level. Staff noted the village has decreased public works budgets recently and Wisconsin remains affordable compared to neighboring states for automobile ownership costs. Motion to adopt passed unanimously.

Michael explained the Ryan Street site recycling program where concrete and asphalt is crushed into base course. King Gravel submitted the low quote at $4.23/ton for an estimated 30,000 tons ($126,900 total). Analysis showed the program saves approximately $11/cubic yard compared to City of Wausau costs, totaling about $130,000 savings on 2026 projects. Motion to award to King Gravel passed unanimously.

Committee discussed enforcement of violations for discharging clear water (primarily sump pumps) into sanitary sewers. After discussion about willful versus unknowing violations, the committee agreed on $100/day fines for violations. Staff will wait for In-pipe Technologies study results, then send notices giving 30-60 days to correct before fines apply. Motion to direct staff to create enforcement language with $100/day maximum passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Water and Sewer Connection Agreement - Radkey Avenue
discussions.body: Michael presented agreement for a new duplex at 2230 Radkey Avenue to temporarily connect via long lateral until Radkey Avenue reconstruction. Committee added requirement that reconstruction occur no later than 2029, at which point property will be assessed for main extension costs. Motion passed unanimously.

Agreement with Village of Rothschild for Military Road reconstruction from Wolfman Street to Helin Street. Weston's share for water and sewer work is $94,860. Steam Construction is the contractor. Motion to approve passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Construction Services for Alderson/Jelinek and Bladel Projects
discussions.body: Michael presented proposals for construction inspection services. For Alderson/Jelinek roundabout: MSA at $37,315 (selected) vs Clark Dietz at $48,910. For Bladel Avenue: Clark Dietz at $73,730 (selected for having full-time experienced staff) vs MSA at $70,400 (using summer intern). Motion to approve both assignments passed unanimously.

actionItems: Staff to add no-later-than-2029 date to Radkey Avenue water/sewer connection agreement
Staff to develop enforcement language for sanitary sewer discharge violations at $100/day fine
Award 2026 recycled asphalt crushing project to King Gravel at $4.23/ton
Approve Village of Rothschild agreement for Military Road construction ($94,860 Weston share)
Award MSA construction services for Alderson/Jelinek roundabout project ($37,315)
Award Clark Dietz construction services for Bladel Avenue project ($73,730)
Schedule special assessment meeting for Bladel Avenue for May 28, 2026 at 6:00 PM
Begin water main flushing next week with notices being mailed
Norcon to begin concrete repairs on Schofield Avenue eastbound lanes starting Wednesday/Thursday
title: Public Works Committee
